title: Reimburse Ikigai Info Governance Action Deposit.

rationale.summary: Vote: ABSTAIN. For this InfoAction (expired), evidence quality was insufficient for a reliable YES or NO decision. Primary blocker: evidence did not clear the directional decision threshold. Decisive reason: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "In September 2024, only weeks after the Chang hard fork to introduce on-chain governance, an Info governance action titled Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai - was submitted." and clears the evidence gates. What would enable a directional vote: Provide independent evidence for: There was significant sentiment at the time that the Ikigai Info governance action deposit should be reimbursed; Provide independent evidence for: It was considered that the submitter should not lose one hundred thousand ada, for being an early pioneer of Cardano governance. Residual uncertainty: Rule-based system; does not infer unstated intent. Confidence context: score 0.01; confidence 42.2%.
rationale.summary_raw: DRep ratification support is above the hard-fork threshold; treated as a positive context signal.
top_fixes: Provide independent evidence for: There was significant sentiment at the time that the Ikigai Info governance action deposit should be reimbursed.
Provide independent evidence for: It was considered that the submitter should not lose one hundred thousand ada, for being an early pioneer of Cardano governance.
Provide independent evidence for: This is a budget Info governance action requesting an amount of 103,000 ada.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Reimburse Ikigai Info Governance Action Deposit.
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: In September 2024, only weeks after the Chang hard fork to introduce on-chain governance, an Info governance action titled Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai - was submitted., This was only a symbolic governance action, as it simply asked the community whether they agreed with a statement thanking those that helped get Cardano to this point and expressing a sense of hope for the future., Unfortunately, due to a bug in the code of the Cardano node that permitted an unregistered stake key to be used in the governance action, the submitter was unable to recover their deposit of 100K ada., While the community expressed at the time the importance of reimbursing this deposit via a treasury withdrawal; once the ability to do so became available, following the Plomin hard fork; unfortunately none of the entity based budget submissions have includ...

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): In September 2024, only weeks after the Chang hard fork to introduce on-chain governance, an Info governance action titled Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai - was submitted.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): This was only a symbolic governance action, as it simply asked the community whether they agreed with a statement thanking those that helped get Cardano to this point and expressing a sense of hope for the future.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): Unfortunately, due to a bug in the code of the Cardano node that permitted an unregistered stake key to be used in the governance action, the submitter was unable to recover their deposit of 100K ada.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): While the community expressed at the time the importance of reimbursing this deposit via a treasury withdrawal; once the ability to do so became available, following the Plomin hard fork; unfortunately none of the entity based budget submissions have includ...
Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): There was significant sentiment at the time that the Ikigai Info governance action deposit should be reimbursed.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): It was considered that the submitter should not lose one hundred thousand ada, for being an early pioneer of Cardano governance.
missing: Independent evidence for: There was significant sentiment at the time that the Ikigai Info governance action deposit should be reimbursed.
Independent evidence for: It was considered that the submitter should not lose one hundred thousand ada, for being an early pioneer of Cardano governance.
Independent evidence for: This is a budget Info governance action requesting an amount of 103,000 ada.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "In September 2024, only weeks after the Chang hard fork to introduce on-chain governance, an Info governance action titled Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai - was submitted." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "There was significant sentiment at the time that the Ikigai Info governance action deposit should be reimbursed." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.
decision_contract.execution_note: Merit is evaluated independently; the executable recommendation also requires verified NCL and portfolio capacity.
